Analysis 1a: Age Distribution of Respond.caption: The sample skews heavily toward 18–24-year-olds (~68%), so findings reflect Gen Z preferences most strongly unless segmented.

Analysis 1b: Gender Distribution of Resp.caption: Respondents are broadly gender-balanced (≈52% female, ≈46% male), reducing the risk that results are driven by a single-gender skew.

Analysis 1c: Annual Household Income Dis.chartTitle: Annual Household Income Distribution of Respondents
Analysis 1c: Annual Household Income Dis.caption: Income skews lower and 30% prefer not to disclose, but there is still a meaningful $100k+ segment (~14%) that aligns with premium purchase intent.

Chart 1: Usage Frequency — How Often Spe.chartTitle: Usage Frequency — How Often Speakers Are Used
Chart 1: Usage Frequency — How Often Spe.caption: Engagement is high: 63.6% use speakers daily or several times/week; these power users comprise 76.5% of high-intent buyers.

Chart 2: Purchase Intent (Next 12 Months.caption: Near-term demand exists but is concentrated: 21.0% are likely (4–5) to buy within 12 months; focus launch on high-intent segments.

Chart 3: Sound Quality Rating Distributi.caption: Most users rate current speaker sound as Good or better (83.6%), but a sizable 'Average' segment (14.8%) indicates room for an audible, premium step-up in fidelity.

Chart 4: Feature Importance Ranking (Low.xAxisLabel: Average Rank (1 = Most Important, 6 = Least Important)
Chart 4: Feature Importance Ranking (Low.caption: Across consumers, Sound Quality ranks as the top priority; durability and battery are key table-stakes, so a hi-fi Beats speaker should lead with audio performance while meeting reliability basics.

Chart 5: Price Paid (Spend Distribution).caption: Most purchases cluster in $50–$200 (58.1%), but there is a premium tail ($200+ = 10.2%) that supports a flagship price when targeted to premium-ready segments.

Chart 6: High Purchase Intent Rate (4-5).chartTitle: High Purchase Intent Rate (4-5) by Household Income
Chart 6: High Purchase Intent Rate (4-5).caption: Likelihood to buy is strongest among premium-ready households; use income targeting to support a higher MSRP without anchoring pricing on the most price-sensitive segments.

Chart 7: Purchase Channel Preference.caption: Consumers prefer buying through large multi-brand retailers (especially online); launch should be digital-first with select retail partners for demos and credibility.

Chart 8: Competitive Penetration — Top S.chartTitle: Competitive Penetration — Top Speaker Brands Owned/Used
Chart 8: Competitive Penetration — Top S.caption: Current ownership/usage is dominated by JBL and Bose; Beats should benchmark against JBL for mass-market scale and Bose for premium credibility, and win via verified performance proof (reviews, comparisons, demos).
